pattern: A permutation array is a direct instruction for where each source character belongs, so writing straight into a freshly allocated buffer at the given destination avoids ever overwriting a value before it is read.
hints: The permutation tells you, for each source position, exactly where its character must land — you don't need to search for anything.
Allocate an output buffer of the same length up front rather than trying to swap characters in place.
For each i, place the character at s[i] into a new array at index indices[i], then join that array back into a string.

edge_inputs.description: identity permutation leaves the string unchanged
non-trivial permutation where a naive in-place swap would clobber a not-yet-read source
repeated letters rely purely on index mapping, not character identity

stress.description: random strings with random permutations of matching length
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, 100)
    s = ''.join(rng.choice('abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz') for _ in range(n))
    indices = list(range(n))
    rng.shuffle(indices)
    return [s, indices]
stress.note: indices is always a true permutation of range(n), matching the problem's guarantee
